growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), pH 7.1 (closed system, no exogenous carbon/energy source, following wash from R2B growth medium)
growth_records.cite: Shoemaker et al. Microbial population dynamics and evolutionary outcomes under extreme energy limitation. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2021). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2101691118. [PMC8379937]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Flavobacterium sp. KBS0721 ... T2 days 75
growth_records.notes: Table 1 T2 = 75 days under 1,000-d energy limitation. doubling_time_h = 75 x 24 = 1800 h; mu = ln2/1800 = 0.000385 h-1. Same conditions/method as other Table 1 entries in this paper.

growth_records.strain: freshwater lake isolate (MN/VA, USA); this study
growth_records.medium: Defined P-rich mineral medium (Tanner, 2007), glucose C source, P-gradient (C:P_R 10-5000)
growth_records.cite: Scott, J Thad et al. Variable Stoichiometry and Homeostatic Regulation of Bacterial Biomass Elemental Composition. Frontiers in Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2012.00042. [PMC3283892]
growth_records.snippet: Chemostat dilution rates were set at 25% μmax for each isolate and operated and sampled as described above.
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (Bacteroidetes). Steady-state chemostat: μ = D = 0.05 h−1 (Table 1). μmax = 0.05/0.25 = 0.20 h−1. td = ln2/0.05 = 13.86 h. Glucose carbon source; no uptake/secretion rates reported. Biomass C:P and homeostasis from Table 1. Temp 25 C; pH not reported.

growth_records.strain: freshwater lake isolate (MN/VA, USA); this study
growth_records.medium: Defined P-rich mineral medium (Tanner, 2007), glucose C source, P-gradient (C:P_R 10-5000)
growth_records.cite: Scott, J Thad et al. Variable Stoichiometry and Homeostatic Regulation of Bacterial Biomass Elemental Composition. Frontiers in Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2012.00042. [PMC3283892]
growth_records.snippet: Chemostat dilution rates were set at 25% μmax for each isolate and operated and sampled as described above.
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (Bacteroidetes). Steady-state chemostat: μ = D = 0.06 h−1 (Table 1). μmax = 0.06/0.25 = 0.24 h−1. td = ln2/0.06 = 11.55 h. Glucose carbon source; no uptake/secretion rates reported. Strongly homeostatic under P deficiency (H'=14.7). Also grown in a co-culture chemostat with Arthrobacter sp. (mixed-culture P-gradient), but no distinct single growth rate is reportable for that mix so it is not a separate record. Biomass C:P from Table 1. Temp 25 C; pH not reported.
